handoff_purpose: Execute the next-step LidLift roadmap with Copilot Fleet using parallel workers and one integration lead.
escalation_policy: Escalate only for blocked third-party credentials, production secrets, or destructive schema changes.

recent_changes: Narrative briefing migrated from direct OpenAI SDK to AI Gateway with devtools enabled only outside production.
Git-backed Vercel production deploys are configured from main with apps/web as root.
Tool discovery support now exists in packages/core/src/discovery.ts and apps/web/app/api/discover-tools/route.ts.
current_head_requirement: Load the repo at current main before making changes.

recommended_next_steps.title: Benchmark harness
recommended_next_steps.objective: Ship a benchmark harness focused on irrelevant-tool refusal, overlapping-tool ranking, and read-vs-write safety mismatch gating.

recommended_next_steps.title: Analytics and telemetry
recommended_next_steps.objective: Instrument user-visible and API-visible telemetry for analyzer runs, guardrail decisions, and narrative usage.

recommended_next_steps.title: Security and dependency triage
recommended_next_steps.objective: Triage the current GitHub vulnerability backlog and add a repeatable security update workflow.

recommended_next_steps.title: Public eval/demo surface
recommended_next_steps.objective: Ship a public eval/demo page with concrete allow/review/clarify/block examples and measurable outcomes.

definition_of_done: All four steps are implemented in code, validated locally, committed on a single branch, and ready for a production deploy from main.

coordination_rule: Workers must not revert each other. If shared-file overlap is unavoidable, LEAD owns the merge.

title: Build benchmark harness
objective: Create a benchmark harness that measures LidLift’s differentiation instead of generic agent performance.
requirements: Add benchmark configs covering three cases: irrelevant tool refusal, overlapping-tool ranking, and read-vs-write mismatch.
Use plain-language scenarios with expected guardrail outcome and best-tool expectations.
Document how to run the benchmark locally and how to feed results back into product evaluation.
validation: Any added benchmark runner or schema validation commands
success_criteria: Benchmark assets exist and are runnable.
Scenarios map directly to LidLift’s allow/review/clarify/block model.
README explains how results should influence future tuning.

title: Instrument analytics and telemetry
objective: Make analyzer usage measurable for product decisions and narrative-layer value assessment.
requirements: Track analyzer submissions, catalog size, final guardrail decision, and whether narrative generation was requested and returned.
Keep telemetry privacy-conscious and avoid storing prompt bodies by default.
Prefer Vercel-native analytics or lightweight deterministic event capture over heavy vendor lock-in.
success_criteria: A run emits enough metadata to answer: how often users analyze, which guardrail decisions are most common, and whether the narrative layer is actually used.
Telemetry code is production-safe and documented in code comments or README where necessary.

title: Triage vulnerabilities and harden update workflow
objective: Reduce current security debt and make ongoing dependency hygiene repeatable.
requirements: Inspect current GitHub vulnerability backlog and remediate what is low-risk to upgrade.
Add GitHub automation for security/dependency awareness.
Document the support/security contact or process.
validation: Any audit or advisory commands you run
success_criteria: At least the low-risk dependency backlog is reduced or explained with pinned blockers.
Security workflow is present in the repo rather than only in chat.
No regression in the web app or MCP server build.

title: Ship public eval/demo surface
objective: Create a shareable proof page that demonstrates why LidLift matters before execution.
requirements: Add a public page that shows representative examples for allow, review, clarify, and block.
Use concrete examples with before/after framing, not generic marketing copy.
Keep the visual style aligned with the current product surface.
success_criteria: The page is crawlable and demo-friendly.
Examples are understandable without reading source code.
At least one example highlights read-vs-write mismatch blocking.

title: Integrate, validate, and prepare release
requirements: Review all worker changes for overlap and coherence.
Run the full local validation sequence.
Run plugin-eval against the repo using the custom metric pack at .plugin-eval/metric-packs/lidlift-next-steps/manifest.json.
Prepare a clean summary of what shipped, what remains blocked, and whether production deploy is safe.
success_criteria: Metric pack output improves from warning-heavy to pass-heavy.
Release notes clearly identify any external blockers such as missing secrets.

engineering_constraints: Do not change the deployed stack split: Vercel web, Cloudflare MCP, packages/core shared logic.
Do not revert existing user changes.
Prefer deterministic local evaluation over subjective prose.
Preserve the current product voice and landing-page quality.
Avoid adding large new infrastructure unless it directly serves one of the four recommended next steps.
non_goals: Do not rebuild the whole architecture.
Do not replace the MCP server transport.
Do not add heavy third-party analytics unless a lightweight native option is insufficient.

release_gate: Do not declare completion until ACC_001 through ACC_005 are all satisfied or explicitly blocked with evidence.

known_blockers.detail: Current shell does not have NOTION_API_TOKEN or NOTION_WORKSPACE_ID; ntn workers list returns 'No workspace selected'.
Ambient API health is live, but no local alpha worker processes were observed and Notion worker status cannot be verified without workspace auth.

instruction: Do not spend Fleet cycles on these external blockers unless credentials are provided during the session.

artifact_outputs: Any new config or benchmark files committed in repo
Optional preview or production deployment notes if validation passes
preferred_commit_strategy: One integration branch with coherent, reviewable commits grouped by worker or by feature area.
